title: Iron sharpens iron. Not online.
caption:
Your Discord server isn't your brothers.

Most Christian guys have a community — a group chat, a server, a men's group that meets twice a year. And they call it brotherhood. Proverbs 27:17 doesn't.

"As iron sharpens iron, so one man sharpens another." Iron against iron creates friction. It throws sparks. It requires contact. You cannot be sharpened by a server.

James 5:16 says confess your sins to each other. Not your prayer requests — your sins. The specific one. That verse assumes one man is in your actual life who knows your story and picks up when you call at midnight.

Online, you control what they see: the verse you posted, the PR you hit, the clean version of the week. A real brother sees the Tuesday at 3 AM version — the one you never post. Physical presence changes what you'll admit. The thing you'd text but not say is the thing that needs to be said.

You don't need a group. You need one man. Someone who asks three things and expects the truth: Are you in the Word? Are you clean? What aren't you telling me?

One man who tells you the truth is worth a thousand who clap.

—

Comment "iron" and I'll send you the link to ETHOS.
